findings.evidence: `rg` shows only `processTail_processCons` at lines 188-193 and no `processCons_processTail`/reverse round-trip lemma.
findings.fix: Add a public `@[simp]` lemma, e.g. `processCons_processTail` proving `processCons (fun ω => t ω 0) (processTail t) = t` by `funext ω n; cases n <;> simp`.
findings.issue: `processCons`/`processTail` expose only `processTail_processCons`; the reverse decomposition `processCons (fun ω => t ω 0) (processTail t) = t` is missing, so consumers cannot state the canonical head/tail reconstruction without reproving it.

findings.evidence: `TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/Basic.lean:103-106` marks `blockLaw_apply_rectangle` as `@[grind =>]`; the new restatement at `Cylinder.lean:71-74` has no annotation.
findings.fix: Annotate `blockLaw_blockCylinder` with `@[grind =>]`, matching `blockLaw_apply_rectangle`, so `grind` can use the cylinder-named restatement without unfolding or manually rewriting through the older set comprehension.
findings.issue: `blockLaw_blockCylinder` is the characteristic bridge from `blockLaw` rectangles to `blockCylinder`, but it lacks the automation annotation used by the underlying rectangle API.

summary: The new surface mostly hides bodies and exposes usable coordinate lemmas, but two characteristic automation/round-trip lemmas are missing from the public API.
